message:
🐛 Fix gtag.js not initializing on Netlify due to MIME type blocking (#2186)

Root cause: On Netlify, /api/gtag returns index.html (SPA catch-all) with
Content-Type: text/html and X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. The browser
fetches it (HTTP 200), fires script.onload, but blocks execution because
the MIME type isn't JavaScript. This caused:
1. markGtagDecided(true) to fire prematurely (no real gtag.js loaded)
2. CDN fallback never triggered (onerror never fires for HTTP 200)
3. Events pushed to dataLayer but never sent to GA4
4. All console.kubestellar.io analytics silently dropped

Fixes:
- After script.onload, verify google_tag_manager is defined (proof gtag.js
  actually initialized) before marking available. If not, try CDN fallback.
- Add Netlify redirect for /api/gtag returning 404, so the browser fires
  onerror immediately and the CDN fallback loads without delay.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -60,6 +60,15 @@
   to = "/.netlify/functions/missions-browse"
   status = 200
 
+# GA4 gtag.js proxy — only exists on Go backend, not Netlify.
+# Return 404 so the browser's onerror fires and the CDN fallback loads.
+# Without this, the SPA catch-all returns index.html (HTML with nosniff),
+# which triggers onload instead of onerror, breaking the CDN fallback chain.
+[[redirects]]
+  from = "/api/gtag"
+  to = "/404.html"
+  status = 404
+
 # GA4 event collection — custom lightweight tracker sends directly to /api/m
 # (Netlify Function via Config.path, no gtag.js loaded)
